mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS relative to the prior month signals easier discounting and higher equity present values, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing periods do not deliver positive median 20d SPX returns or if forward-return sign accuracy falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the market is in a restrictive rate regime that compresses valuation multiples and raises recession risk, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the signal loses negative sign consistency for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor demand and falling earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of triggered episodes or if the hit rate is not below 45% across rolling 24-month samples.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE, with payroll growth slowing while unemployment rises, confirms labor-market weakening and should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE condition does not outperform either series alone and if triggered episodes fail to produce negative 20d returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and nominal revenue growth, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not correspond to positive median 20d SPX returns or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals housing-led demand weakness and a broader slowdown in cyclical activity, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing weakness is followed by positive 60d SPX returns more than 55% of the time or if the signal disappears after controlling for the broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL remains contained, it indicates real activity is improving without an inflation shock, so equity returns over the next 5 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-CPI combination does not outperform INDPRO alone and if positive 5d SPX returns are not more frequent during joint positive-production low-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary recovery, rising INDPRO combined with stable or falling CPIAUCSL lowers real-rate pressure and supports a sustained equity rerating, implying positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL do not deliver positive 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of instances or if the effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is stable.
